retrieved_result: What the Triangle Shirtwaist fire means for workers now Mourners honor victims of New York's 1911 Triangle Shirtwaist Company fire at the Mount Richmond Cemetery in Staten Island on March 1. 22 of the fire's 146 casualties were buried in the cemetery. (David Karp/ASSOCIATED PRESS)By Hilda L. Solis March 18, 2011A century ago this week, in Lower Manhattan
Decision_Reason: The retrieved page discusses a historical fire and workers' memorials, unrelated to software development or IDEs.
